cqs_version: 1.26.1+post (Phase-1 through Phase-5b on main; Phase-5c PR #1020 + Phase-5d pending)

hardware.secondary_gpu: Quadro RTX 4000 (8 GB, idle during measurement)

corpus.name: cqs codebase
corpus.languages: rust + python (eval scripts) + markdown + a few stragglers

index_size.index_db_human: 2.4 GB (includes content, summaries, hyde, embeddings as BLOBs)
index_size.notes: DB dominated by enrichments (LLM summaries + HyDE). HNSW alone is ~4.7 KB/chunk. Pre-enrichment indexes are far smaller.

latency_ms.notes: CLI cold = full process spawn + ONNX init + DB open + HNSW load. Daemon mode amortizes all of this.

throughput.batch_composition: 20 search + 10 callers + 5 callees + 6 impact + 4 explain + 4 scout + 1 misc
throughput.notes: Throughput dominated by search ops (200ms each). Pure graph-op throughput is much higher — callers alone runs at ~10 ops/sec via daemon.

post_v1_26_1_perf_changes: #917 streaming SPLADE serialize — eliminates ~5-10 MB peak memory duplication on default ensembledistil model (~60-100 MB on SPLADE-Code 0.6B)
#966 stream-hash enrichment — drops ~100 MB allocator pressure on a 100k-chunk reindex
#969 recency-based last_indexed_mtime prune — eliminates O(n) stat() syscalls on WSL watch path
